metadata.scope: Beginner-friendly readings of notation used throughout CO 250.

entries.title: Real vector
entries.meaning: x is a list of n real numbers. Its entries may be negative, zero, positive, or fractional.

entries.title: Integer vector
entries.meaning: Every entry of x must be a whole number.

entries.title: Binary variable
entries.meaning: y records a yes/no choice: zero usually means off or no, and one means on or yes.

entries.title: Componentwise nonnegativity
entries.meaning: Every entry satisfies x_j≥0. It does not mean that x is one scalar number.

entries.title: Membership and containment
entries.readAloud: x belongs to S; S one is contained in S two
entries.meaning: The first statement says x is an element of S. The second says every point in S1 is also in S2.

entries.title: Transpose
entries.meaning: Rows and columns switch roles. If A is m-by-n, then A transpose is n-by-m.

entries.title: Dot product
entries.meaning: Multiply matching entries of c and x, then add. LP objectives are often written this way.

entries.title: A system of inequalities
entries.readAloud: A x is at most b
entries.meaning: This is shorthand for one inequality per row of A. The comparison is componentwise.

entries.title: One matrix row
entries.readAloud: row i of A times x is at most b i
entries.meaning: This is the i-th individual constraint inside Ax≤b.

entries.title: Optimization in set-builder form
entries.readAloud: maximize c transpose x subject to A x at most b and x nonnegative
entries.meaning: The expression before the colon is the score. Everything after the colon is a rule that feasible choices must satisfy.

entries.title: Set of minimizers
entries.readAloud: arg min of f over x in S
entries.meaning: This returns the point or points that achieve the smallest value, not the smallest value itself.

entries.title: Optimal value
entries.meaning: This is the best objective value. It is different from an optimal solution x, which is a decision vector.

entries.title: Euclidean norm
entries.meaning: This is the ordinary straight-line length of vector x.

entries.title: Convex hull
entries.meaning: The smallest convex set containing S: imagine filling every line segment between points in S.

entries.title: Generated cone
entries.readAloud: the cone generated by a one through a k
entries.meaning: Take nonnegative multiples of the listed vectors and add them.

entries.title: Gradient and subgradient set
entries.readAloud: the gradient of g at x bar; the subdifferential of g at x bar
entries.meaning: The gradient is one slope vector when g is differentiable. The subdifferential is the set of all valid subgradients.

entries.title: Common logic symbols
entries.readAloud: for every; there exists; implies; if and only if
entries.meaning: An implication gives one direction. If and only if means both directions must be proved.

entries.title: Augmented matrix
entries.meaning: The vertical bar is a visual divider, not a mathematical operation. The columns of A hold coefficients and the last column holds the right-hand side b.

entries.title: Basis matrix
entries.meaning: Keep from A exactly the columns whose indices are in the basis B. In simplex, these columns must form an invertible square matrix.

entries.title: Active-row matrix
entries.meaning: Keep from A exactly the constraint rows indexed by I, often the constraints that are tight at the point being studied.

entries.title: Matrix rank
entries.meaning: The number of independent row directions, equivalently independent column directions. Rank tells us how many genuinely different linear conditions the matrix imposes.
